title: Kadapa city under siege for twenty years, decries MLA Madhavi Reddy
content: Government whip and Kadapa MLA Reddeppagari Madhavi Reddy said the city had remained underdeveloped for 20 years, blaming earlier civic administrations, after flagging off a Census awareness rally

title: Census 2027: self-enumeration window open till April 30, public urged to use online portal
content: NTR District Joint Collector S. Ilakkiya flagged off a 5-km run in Vijayawada to spread awareness about the Census 2027 self-enumeration form, which can be filled online until April 30

title: Assembly Elections 2026 LIVE: Trinamool's defeat is imminent, says PM Modi in West Bengal rally
content: Even smallest of politicians, goons consider themselves the government under TMC regime: PM at poll rally in Bongaon

title: Shivamogga MP urges people not to panic over nuclear power plant, says no such proposal
content: The MP also noted that people displaced in the Sharavathi project have not yet received adequate compensation

title: Raghu Rai didn’t just photograph India, he invented a way of seeing it
content: The audacity of Raghu Rai’s wide-angle lens, the tension he seemed to choreograph at the edges of the frame added a cinematic aside long before cinema caught up with that language.

title: ‘BJP’s Bill in effect was to reserve seats for men’, alleges Congress spokesperson
content: The BJP, which came to power in 2014, brought the Bill to Parliament only in 2023 — and even then, attached conditions of census and delimitation

title: Home Ministry-level talks with Ladakh’s LAB, KDA to resume on May 22
content: Amit Shah to visit Ladakh soon; the Ladakh-Centre talks hit a stalemate after violence erupted in Leh in September last year, which left four locals dead and over 80, including security personnel, injured

title: Exhibition in Palakkad celebrates ornithologist Induchoodan
content: An exhibition of photographs of Kerala’s birds was organised in collaboration with the Induchoodan Foundation

title: IMD issues heat alert in several regions of Maharashtra and thunderstorm alert in a few regions
content: According to the IMD, Mumbai, Thane, Raigad, Akola, Vidarbha, Wardha, and Ratnagiri are among the districts under a heat alert, where the heat and humidity will increase

title: Karnataka submits 1,082 urban development projects to the Centre costing ₹1.25 lakh crore: DKS
content: The proposed Skydeck project in Bengaluru has faced hurdles due to restrictions linked to HAL Airport, says Dy CM D.K. Shivakumar

title: Forest fires spread across Nilgiris; helicopters help battle blaze
content: Forest department officials believe that the fires were started by people, possibly in retaliation against the forest department for their perceived lack of action after the killing of three persons by tigers in The Nilgiris over the last year.

title: Nrusimha Sagar reservoir non-operational due to pending compensation to displaced families: BJP
content: Gudur Narayana Reddy said though the project was completed in 2023, it has been lying vacant without catering to the needs of downstream areas

title: Raghu Rai | 1942-2026
content: Raghu Rai’s story is intricately linked with that of India. Without either, the story would be incomplete

title: Tourist bitten by snake near Cherai beach in Kerala’s Kochi; under observation in MICU
content: The incident comes amid reports of a seasonal uptick in snakebite cases across Kerala amid the scorching summer

title: Tension as alleged BJP workers disrupt Malayalam poet Kureepuzha Sreekumar’s speech at Pathanamthitta
content: The poet was addressing a gathering after receiving an award instituted by the Nellickal Muraleedharan Foundation

title: Court can reject anticipatory bail but cannot direct accused to surrender: Supreme Court
content: "If the court wants to reject the anticipatory bail, it may do so, but the court has no jurisdiction to say that the petitioner should now surrender," says Bench of Justices

title: BJP committed to women's security, Trinamool ‘stands with goons who torture women’: PM Modi
content: “The TMC’s ‘nirmam sarkar’ (cruel government) stands with goons who torture women of this State. The time has now come to say we will tolerate no more,” Modi said

title: TMC's reign of terror prevails in West Bengal: Rahul Gandhi
content: Debdeep Chatterjee was brutally murdered in Asansol during post-poll violence. He was closely associated with Asansol North Congress candidate Prasenjit Puitandi, the party claims

title: Virudhunagar Collector warns fireworks units of license cancellation for non-cooperation during inspections
content: Some fireworks units remained closed with their gates locked on April 25, 2026 preventing officials from conducting inspections of safety measures

title: NCB seizes 474 soap cases of heroin worth ₹11 crore in Manipur
content: Three arrested in joint operation; the agency says it has been intensifying its operations in Manipur due to its proximity to international borders and exposure to transnational drug trafficking routes

title: NHAI fast-tracks alignment identification for 35 km-long Mangaluru Bypass
content: NHAI has appointed M/S L.N. Malviya Infra Projects Pvt. Ltd. as consultant for preparing a Detailed Project Report for the proposed bypass project

title: Use knowledge for progress, be job creators: Vice-President to youth
content: Radhakrishnan stresses innovation, ethical conduct and women’s empowerment at Rajasthan University convocation

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Over 50 lakh more people voted this time compared to 2024 Lok Sabha polls
content: In the State, people’s interest in polling process has always been higher for Assembly elections when compared to Lok Sabha polls, show data from elections held over the past 20 years; parties carry out a more intensive mobilisation for State polls

title: AAP submits petition to Rajya Sabha Chairman seeking termination of seven MPs who quit party
content: AAP MP Sanjay Singh claimed the move by the seven Rajya Sabha MPs amounted to defection as it was against the provisions of the anti-defection law

title: The real contest is between the BJP and Congress, not with Trinamool, says Rahul Gandhi
content: Congress leader tries to paint the BJP and Trinamool with the same brush; Mamata-ji did in West Bengal what Narendra Modi has done in the country, he says

title: Gujarat local body polls: Amit Shah, Gujarat CM Bhupendra Patel cast vote
content: The elections are being held for 15 municipal corporations, including Ahmedabad, Surat, Vadodara and Rajkot, 84 municipalities, 34 district panchayats and 260 taluka panchayats, making it one of the largest electoral exercises in the State.

title: Raghav Chadha, six AAP Rajya Sabha MPs quit party, set to merge with BJP
content: MPs Raghav Chadha, Sandeep Pathak, and Ashok Kumar Mittal met BJP president Nitin Nabin at the party’s headquarters

title: Azim Premji University to host Sahitya Sahavasa in Mysuru on April 28
content: The day-long programme to be inaugurated by former Vice Chancellor of Sanskrit University Dr. Padma Shekar will celebrate the literary contributions of eminent Kannada writers

title: Illegal sex determination tests in Mandya: DC issues directions for inspections of all scanning centres in district
content: The lapses by the private diagnostic centres including their failure to maintain proper records of the tests conducted by them had been shared with the officials

title: Nashik godman Ashok Kharat case: What we know so far
content: Several political leaders and social activists have accused Ashok Kharat of having alleged connections with prominent politicians and bureaucrats in the State

title: West Bengal Assembly polls: Matua first family split deepens battle in Bagda, Gaighata as SIR jolts BJP bastions
content: The battle now runs through the divided Matua first family, with brothers, wives and cousins contesting from rival camps, while the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls has stirred fresh anxieties among refugee Hindus who once voted together

title: A spectacle like no other, Thrissur Pooram mesmerises audiences
content: All major rituals have been retained, the Kudamattam will be curtailed to 15 minutes in the wake of Mundathikode fire accident

title: Experts recommend regular breaks from screens to reduce impact on mental health
content: Rather than forcefully taking away gadgets from children, the process to break their screen-use habits must be done gradually, they say

title: Kidwai’s doorstep palliative care sees steady uptake; no-cost services reach 112 patients
content: The service currently covers Bengaluru within a radius of about 40 km from the institute and is delivered through two dedicated teams conducting daily home visits

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: officials mull increasing vote counting tables for Paravur amid fear of delay in result
content: Officials had earlier decided to set up 13 counting tables at the centre, citing space constraints inside the counting hall. Most of the other constituencies will have 14 tables, it is learnt.

title: Jaladhara-Jalaharathi: silt-choked Toorpu Vanka feeder canal revived in Sambavaram
content: Officials say the Toorpu Vanka feeder canal in Sambavaram, silted up over the years, is being desilted under MGNREGS at a cost of ₹2.94 lakh

title: Srisailam temple EO asks choultries to set up drinking water kiosks, ensure hygiene at annadanam
content: The Sri Bhramaramba Mallikarjuna Swamy temple at Srisailam has asked choultry managements to set up drinking water kiosks for summer and serve free buttermilk to pilgrims, the EO said

title: 20 illegal immigrants apprehended in Assam, pushed back to Bangladesh
content: "Rude people don't understand soft language. We continuously remind ourselves of this prophetic line when we expel infiltrators from Assam who don't leave themselves. For instance, these 20 illegal Bangladeshis who were PUSHED BACK last night," says Mr. Sarma

title: Light rains over isolated places in Ghat districts, south coastal districts of Tamil Nadu from April 26 to May 1
content: On April 27, isolated places over the Western Ghat districts, Delta districts and South Coastal districts of Tamil Nadu and Karaikal area are likely to get light to moderate rains.

title: TTD invites applications for S.V. Balamandiram admissions for orphaned children 2026-27
content: TTD has opened admissions to S.V. Balamandiram for orphaned Hindu children aged five to 10. Applications close on May 16, 2026

title: Panic buying shuts 421 petrol bunks in Andhra Pradesh; CM Naidu orders review
content: Panic buying pushed Andhra Pradesh’s petrol and diesel sales 50% above the daily average on Saturday, shutting 421 of 4,510 outlets

title: Adani Green to invest ₹15,000 crore to add 10 gigawatt battery storage in FY27
content: The proposed addition will be over and above the roughly three GWh of installed storage capacity the company expects to reach shortly, following the commissioning of 1.4 GWh during FY26, it said during its earnings call

title: Engineering student harassed in Vijayawada auto, accused booked after Sakthi App alert
content: The Sakthi App came to the rescue of the student on the National Highway between Vijayawada and Guntur, said Mangalagiri DSP Ch. Murali Krishna

title: 1,000 days of Annavaasal: Erode’s dusk meal mission feeds over 2.5 lakh people
content: Every evening between 6 p.m. and 6.30 p.m., volunteers serve idlis with kurma. Depending on sponsors’ contributions, items such as chapati, tomato rice, fruits, biscuits, and eggs are also provided. On average, about 250 people are fed daily

title: Tagged Olive Ridley turtles from Tamil Nadu reach Sri Lanka Dome, reveal migration patterns
content: Considered one of the most productive regions in the Indian Ocean, the Sri Lanka Dome phenomenon typically begins in May, peaks in July, and declines by September

title: India, New Zealand to sign FTA on April 27; deal provides Indian exporters 100% duty-free access
content: The FTA between the two countries is one of the fastest-negotiated trade agreements that India has entered into, with negotiations being announced in March 2025 and concluded in December that same year.

title: Zurich-bound Swiss plane aborts take-off at Delhi airport
content: In a detailed statement, an airline spokesperson said there were 228 passengers and four infants on board and six passengers are undergoing medical evaluation

title: Interview | BJP will form govt. on its own strength this time in Bengal, says senior leader Tapas Roy
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee’s one-point agenda is to remain in power, and for that she is willing to do anything, claims former Minister Tapas Roy, now candidate from Maniktala seat in north Kolkata.

title: 'Ran towards ballroom': Volunteer recalls WH gunman assembled 'long weapon' in 'makeshift room'
content: A White House Correspondents' Dinner volunteer reported the suspected gunman assembled a "long weapon" in an unsecured area near a terrace entrance before the attack. The suspect then moved toward the ballroom, firing shots and causing panic among guests and staff. The gunman, identified as Cole Allen, was arrested after allegedly entering the Washington Hilton hotel.

title: Hilton stay, zero checks: How shooter breached WH dinner security
content: An armed man breached security at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, exchanging fire with law enforcement before being apprehended. Investigators believe gaps in outer security layers allowed the suspect, staying at the hotel, to approach the event. He was tackled by Secret Service agents near a checkpoint.

title: More than a dozen threats & assassination attempts — Timeline of attacks on Trump
content: A shooting attempt at the White House Correspondents' Dinner led to the immediate evacuation of President Trump and other officials. This incident adds to a long list of threats and security scares faced by Trump, including armed intruders, assassination plots, and poison mail, making him a frequent target.

title: '28 days of hell': Meta employees brace for layoffs as 8,000 jobs get cut on May 20
content: Meta is cutting roughly 8,000 employees—10% of its workforce—on May 20, with another 6,000 open roles being closed. The announcement, made via an internal memo from chief people officer Janelle Gale, has sent workers into a monthlong limbo. Employees are flooding internal forums and the anonymous app Blind with anxiety, dark humor, and resignation. The layoffs are tied to Meta's aggressive AI spending, projected at up to $135 billion this year.

title: West Bengal elections: PM Modi pledges citizenship for Matua Namasudras under CAA
content: PM Modi also offered prayers at Thakurbari, the main temple of the Matua Mahasangha at Thakurnagar in West Bengal's North 24 Parganas district before his poll rally in the area. His visit to the headquarters of the community, housing the shrines of the sect's founders Hari Chand and Guru Chand Thakur, is seen as an outreach to Matua voters who influence at least 34 assembly seats directly and another two dozen along the Bangladesh border.

title: After backing Trump's child savings plan, Michael Dell's next $750 m going to hospital
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Data over dynasties': Grok picks Modi over Rahul in hypothetical vote for PM
content: Grok, the AI chatbot, has sparked renewed debate by hypothetically stating it would vote for Prime Minister Narendra Modi if it were an Indian citizen. The chatbot cited economic growth, infrastructure, and digital transformation as reasons for its preference, contrasting this with Rahul Gandhi's focus on welfare critiques.

title: 'Aaj sirf ... ': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi spills dressing-room talk with Yashasvi
content: Teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashed a blistering 103 off 37 balls, his second IPL century, after a dressing-room pact with Yashasvi Jaiswal to attack. Despite his explosive knock, Rajasthan Royals' 228 was chased down. Sooryavanshi's performance earned praise from opposition captain Pat Cummins, who called him his 'new favourite player'.

title: India, New Zealand to lock in FTA next week: Here's what it may have in store
content: India and New Zealand are set to sign a Free Trade Agreement on April 27, aiming to boost bilateral trade to $5 billion within five years. The pact will offer duty-free access for many Indian exports, benefiting sectors like Agra's leather industry. New Zealand gains tariff reductions on key products, while India protects sensitive agricultural goods.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 39: Ranveer Singh film chases 'Pushpa 2’s domestic record
content: On Day 39, Ranveer Singh’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is on the cusp of making history as it chases the worldwide record of SS Rajamouli’s 'Baahubali 2'. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the espionage thriller has reached a global gross of Rs. 1,772.94 crore, needing just Rs. 15.12 crore more to become the second highest-grossing Indian film ever. Domestically, the film is locked in an intense battle to dethrone 'Pushpa 2: The Rule' as the all-time India net champion.

title: Lungi Ngidi's first words after horrific injury in DC vs PBKS clash
content: Lungi Ngidi has reassured fans about his recovery following a frightening head and neck injury sustained during an IPL 2026 match. The South African pacer, discharged from a Delhi hospital, expressed gratitude for the support.  Despite the injury scare, the Delhi Capitals bowler is reportedly doing well.

title: MS Dhoni's record broken; Sanju Samson goes past CSK legend
content: Sanju Samson achieved a significant milestone, crossing 5000 runs in the IPL during Chennai Super Kings' match against Gujarat Titans. He became one of the fastest batters to reach this landmark, joining an elite group of players.  The match also saw Gujarat Titans win the toss and opt to bowl first.

title: Orphaned twice: SIR strips Kolkata’s Kerala-born man of vote, identity
content: An orphan who found a family and voted for the first time in the 2024 Lok Sabha polls has been disenfranchised. The Election Commission removed his name from the electoral roll after he couldn't provide details of his biological parents, severing his formal link with his foster guardian.

title: Viral video at White House shooting shows press members ‘stealing’ wine amid chaos
content: A scare at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where "shots fired" caused panic and evacuation, has been overshadowed by a viral video. Footage shows attendees, identified as press members, taking wine bottles from tables as the event cleared. This has sparked a heated online debate, with reactions ranging from casual acceptance to strong condemnation of alleged theft.

title: Flight, train fares to Kolkata soar as voters rush home to cast ballots
content: Voters are rushing home to Kolkata for the second phase of elections, causing air and train fares to skyrocket. Fear of names disappearing from electoral rolls has led to a surge in last-minute bookings, with many making expensive one-day return trips solely to cast their vote.

title: Trump survives White House dinner shooting scare as armed 'tutor' held — key points
content: A shooting scare near the White House Correspondents' Dinner forced the evacuation of President Trump and other top officials. The suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was apprehended after allegedly charging a security checkpoint with multiple weapons. An officer was shot but saved by a bulletproof vest. Investigations into the motive are ongoing.

title: 'I know all your secrets': Ishan Kishan reveals chat with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Ishan Kishan reflects on his challenging period out of the Indian team, emphasising his focus on consistent domestic run-scoring to earn a recall. He highlights his determination and hunger, which led to impressive performances. Kishan also discusses his confidence batting at No. 3 and his approach to pacing an innings, as demonstrated in Sunrisers Hyderabad's recent chase.

title: Are Trump, Netanyahu in sync? How US and Israel are fighting different wars on same battlefield
content: As a two-month conflict with Iran escalates, cracks are appearing in the US-Israel alliance. While both nations initially targeted Tehran, their post-strike objectives diverge, with Washington seeking negotiation and Jerusalem aiming for regime change. This fundamental difference, exacerbated by past events and differing pressures, raises questions about their long-term alignment.

title: Chernobyl anniversary: Zelenskyy accuses Russia of 'once again bringing world to brink of disaster'
content: Marking Chernobyl's 40th anniversary, Ukraine's President Zelenskyy accused Russia of "nuclear terrorism," citing drone activity near the plant and warning of man-made disasters. Meanwhile, Ukrainian drones struck a Russian fertilizer plant, causing a pipeline leak. Russia also launched numerous drones overnight, with Ukraine intercepting most.

title: No hard decoupling: India balances China trade with export push
content: India is strategically balancing its trade with China, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing and exports while reducing reliance on Chinese inputs. The nation is building resilient supply chains and increasing its export capacity, though a complete decoupling remains unfeasible. Efforts are underway to diversify sourcing and address the widening trade deficit.

title: 'I've studied assassinations': Trump on why White House dinner shooting keeps happening to him
content: Following a shooting scare at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, President Trump stated he has studied assassinations and believes impactful leaders are often targeted. He linked the incident to his presidency's influence, suggesting those unhappy with changes are responsible. Trump also commented on the personal risks of political life and criticized the hotel's security.

title: Voting in Bengal, help-less in Gurgaon: On ‘10-min’ apps, wait time for slots is 3-5 days
content: An unprecedented exodus of domestic workers to West Bengal for elections has created a severe shortage in Gurgaon, straining instant help services. Wait times for bookings have extended to several days, and available workers are facing doubled workloads and longer hours to meet the surge in demand.

title: AAP sends petition to Rajya Sabha Chair to terminate 7 MPs who moved to BJP
content: The Aam Aadmi Party has urged the Rajya Sabha Chairman to disqualify seven MPs who defected to the BJP, citing constitutional experts who believe they are liable for disqualification. This move follows the MPs' departure, sparking a legal and political dispute over anti-defection rules.

title: Gujarat horror: Dietician killed by husband in Surat, body kept in wooden box
content: A dietician was allegedly murdered by her husband in Surat, Gujarat, with her body discovered stuffed inside a wooden box at his old residence. The accused husband had filed a missing person report for his wife before disappearing himself. Police are actively searching for the suspect and investigating the motive.

title: Drill, sanction, control: Inside the oil economics driving Trump 2.0
content: US President Trump's second term prioritizes oil, leveraging energy dominance for global influence. Campaign funding from oil interests aligns with policies boosting domestic production and easing regulations. Sanctions on rivals and geopolitical events like the Hormuz crisis engineer demand for American exports, impacting nations like India and reshaping global energy markets.

title: 'Plays like gully cricket': Kaif on Sooryavanshi's blazing century
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, aged 15, has earned high praise from Mohammad Kaif for his explosive 103 off 37 balls in IPL 2026. Kaif lauded the youngster's fearless, aggressive approach against top bowlers, calling him a rare, once-in-a-generation talent.  His consistent high strike-rate and mature batting suggest a bright future for Indian cricket.

title: Market recap: Rs 2 lakh crore wiped out from 7 of top-10 firms; TCS biggest laggard
content: Indian equity markets experienced a significant downturn, with the top ten most-valued firms losing over Rs 2 lakh crore. Tata Consultancy Services and Reliance Industries bore the brunt of this decline, influenced by geopolitical tensions and weak IT sector commentary. The broader market also saw substantial drops in benchmark indices.

title: 'If you're not close enough, your photo is not good enough': Remembering Raghu Rai
content: Legendary photographer Raghu Rai, who shaped India's visual memory for over five decades, passed away on Sunday. His iconic images, from the Bhopal Gas tragedy to portraits of national figures, gave permanence to history. Rai believed visual history was more important than fine art, emphasizing closeness to truth in his immersive photographic practice.

title: Punjab Police withdraw Harbhajan Singh’s security cover after BJP switch
content: Security has been reportedly withdrawn from Rajya Sabha MP Harbhajan Singh, who recently switched from AAP to BJP.  This move follows protests by AAP workers, who labelled the defecting MPs as 'traitors'.  Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann accused the BJP of attempting to destabilize AAP and betraying Punjab's mandate.  The seven MPs cited ideological differences for their departure.

title: 'Shots will be fired': Karoline Leavitt’s pre-dinner comments go viral after White House shooting
content: A shooting scare at the White House Correspondents' Dinner saw President Trump evacuated after a suspect armed with multiple weapons breached a security checkpoint.  Secret Service agents engaged the individual, with one officer sustaining non-critical injuries. The suspect, identified as Cole Tomas Allen, was apprehended. Trump later described the event as an attempted assassination.

title: Ashwin rallies behind Nair after costly drops, says 'I’m not making excuses'
content: Punjab Kings achieved a record IPL chase, but Karun Nair's fielding errors proved costly. He dropped Shreyas Iyer twice, crucial misses that allowed Iyer to score a match-winning 71. Despite the blunders, Ravichandran Ashwin defended Nair, suggesting fatigue and a sudden game entry might have contributed to his struggles.

title: India’s self-medication habit: You are not just treating a headache, you could be harming your liver
content: A silent health crisis is unfolding in India as self-medication with over-the-counter painkillers, antibiotics, and supplements leads to acute liver failure. Dr. Kavya Harika Dendukuri highlights that paracetamol, NSAIDs, and unregulated supplements are major culprits, often causing irreversible damage before symptoms appear. This widespread practice, fueled by a lack of awareness, puts millions at risk.

title: TCS Nashik probe runs into hurdles as accused has different passwords for each file and app
content: Police are facing challenges in a sexual assault and religious coercion probe linked to a Nashik TCS office. The prime accused is using multiple passwords and Face ID on his cellphone, hindering access to crucial digital evidence. Forensic experts are assisting investigators to unlock the device, which is key to the ongoing investigation into nine registered cases.

title: 'We’ve got some work': Ponting flags concerns despite unbeaten PBKS run
content: Ricky Ponting hailed Shreyas Iyer’s leadership after Punjab Kings pulled off a record chase of 265 against Delhi Capitals, with Iyer anchoring the win with an unbeaten 71. He praised the strong start by Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya, calling their partnership key. Ponting said the team draws confidence from Iyer’s presence in chases, but added that bowling and fielding still need improvement despite the historic victory.

title: 'Zoomed camera focusing on legs': Drunk flyer films female crew on Hyderabad–Udaipur flight
content: An IndiGo passenger bound for Udaipur was apprehended at Hyderabad airport for allegedly photographing female cabin crew inappropriately and harassing a co-passenger. The businessman, reportedly intoxicated, was deboarded after ignoring warnings. Police have registered a case under the new penal code for insulting a woman's modesty.

title: ‘I just want to go home’: Tearful Erika Kirk escorted out after White House dinner
content: Erika Kirk was seen being escorted from the White House Correspondents' Dinner in tears after gunfire caused panic and evacuation. The incident evoked sympathy due to her husband Charlie Kirk's death last year. The dinner was disrupted by a shooting near the security screening area, leading to a lockdown and the arrest of a suspect.

title: Heated! Babar Azam vs umpire steals the show in PSL - Watch
content: Peshawar Zalmi captain Babar Azam was seen in a heated exchange with the umpire during a PSL match against Lahore Qalandars. Two separate ball changes were necessitated within the first three overs due to boundaries, leading to Azam's visible frustration. This unusual occurrence early in the innings clearly unsettled the skipper.

title: Russian Defence Minister In North Korea, To Hold Talks With Top Leadership
content: Belousov will hold talks with North Korea's top leadership and senior military officials and take part in "ceremonial and commemorative events."

title: "Saw Trump's Resilient Side": Indian Journalist Who Attended US Event To NDTV
content: Saurabh Shukla, editor-in-chief of Newsmobile, credited the US Secret Service for successfully prevent the attacker from entering the ballroom where the event was underway.

title: "I Want To Go Home": Erica Kirk In Tears After White House Dinner Shooting
content: The moment has captured an attention especially after her husband, Charlie Kirk, an ally of Trump, was shot dead in September last year while speaking at Utah Valley University.

title: 'Ma' Crying, 'Maati' With Infiltrators, 'Maanush' Scared: PM Modi's Bengal Speech
content: 2026 West Bengal Elections: 'Ma' Crying, 'Maati' With Infiltrators, 'Maanush' Scared: PM Modi's Bengal Speech

title: Karoline Leavitt's Unwitting Prophecy Came True As 'Shots Fired' At Trump Event
content: "He is ready to rumble, I will tell you. This speech tonight will be classic Donald J Trump," White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said

title: UK In Touch With US Ahead Of King Charles' Visit Amid Trump Security Breach
content: The British government and Buckingham Palace took the security of King Charles "very seriously", an official said after Saturday's incident

title: Gujarat Local Body Polls: Adani Airport Director Jeet Adani, Wife Cast Vote
content: The couple arrived at the polling station and exercised their democratic right amid ongoing voting in the state.

title: Is Virality Replacing Storytelling? Experts Weigh In At NDTV Yuva 2026
content: Virality, short attention spans, and changing reading habits are shaping how young people consume and create content in today's digital world.

title: How Trump's 2018 Iran Nuclear Deal Exit Is Creating Problems For Him In 2026
content: Soon after the US's exit, Iran began stepping away from limits that kept its nuclear work under control.

title: "Army Doesn't Get Business": US Soldier Accused Of Betting On Maduro Operation
content: The prosecutors alleged that his last bet was placed hours before the US Special Forces apprehended Maduro.

title: Trump Was Going To "Rip It" With Media, Then Literal Shots Were Fired
content: The missed opportunity at WHCD, often called "Nerd Prom", comes at a time New York Times found the disapproval for Trump to be at its highest during his second term in office.

title: Kerala State Bus Fined Rs 30,000 For Denying Seat To Ailing Passenger
content: The action came on a complaint filed by Thottassheriyara native, who had boarded a Kerala State Road Transport Corporation (KSRTC) bus from Amballur in Thrissur to Kolappuram in Malappuram.

title: Half-Sleeves, Shorts: Mumbai's New Dress Code For Inmates To Prevent Suicides
content: Data shows that between 2020 and 2023, more than 54 custodial deaths were reported in Maharashtra, with several cases involving suicide by hanging.

title: "Gas Connection Will Be Cut Tonight": How Mumbaikars Were Duped Amid Mideast Crisis
content: The Mahanagar Gas Limited (MGL) customers said the scammers sent them messages to pay their pending bills.

title: Journalists' Posts From Trump Event Spark 'Safe Or Unsafe Venue' Debate
content: Trump event shooting: Donald Trump initially said it was "not a particularly secure building," but later said the ballroom where the event was being held was not breached by the gunman and was "very, very secure"

title: At NDTV Yuva, Galgotias University Students Share Ideas On AI-Driven Automation
content: At the event, students from Galgotias University presented ideas which ranged from AI-driven automation and cybersecurity to farm monitoring systems and crop disease detection tools.

title: Palestinians Vote In First Gaza Local Elections In Over 20 Years
content: Though turnout was low, voters said they were driven to the polls amid a near-total absence of public services.

title: 300-Year-Old $10 Million Violin Stolen By Nazis Likely Found In France
content: A rare Stradivarius violin, the "Joachim-Ma Stradivarius", fetched $11.3 million at auction in New York in February.

title: Trump Evacuated After Gunshots At Event: A Look At Past Threats, Attempts
content: This isn't the first time Trump has faced such a situation, as he had met with two assassination attempts during the 2024 campaign.
